timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's 2024 US election and Trump criminal trial coverage (per user instruction). User-provided URLs were heavily hush-money-trial-focused; supplemented with AJ's broader 2024 campaign coverage to cover the campaign side of the subtopic ('Trump campaign criminal trials'). Contributing articles (hush money sequence): https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/4/15/in-trumps-new-york-hush-money-case-prosecutors-push-election-angle ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/4/15/five-takeaways-from-first-day-of-trumps-historic-new-york-cirminal-trial ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/4/30/trumps-new-york-hush-money-trial-to-resume-with-more-testimony-in-third-week ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/5/2/five-takeaways-from-day-10-of-donald-trumps-new-york-hush-money-trial ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/5/28/new-york-jury-hearing-closing-arguments-as-trump-hush-money-trial-nears-end ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2024/5/30/trump-hush-money-trial-live-news-jury-to-deliver-verdict-in-new-york-case ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/5/30/new-york-jury-finds-donald-trump-guilty-in-new-york-hush-money-trial ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/5/31/trump-first-ex-president-ever-convicted-of-a-crime-slams-new-york-trial . Additional contributing articles drawn from AJ's broader 2024 election coverage: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/1/us-supreme-court-rules-trump-has-some-immunity-in-election-case ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/13/donald-trump-shot-during-pennsylvania-rally ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/15/us-judge-dismisses-classified-documents-case-against-donald-trump ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/18/trump-formally-nominated-republican-presidential-candidate ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/21/joe-biden-drops-out-of-2024-presidential-election ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/10/trump-harris-presidential-debate ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/15/trump-faces-second-apparent-assassination-attempt-at-his-florida-golf-course ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/18/judge-delays-trumps-hush-money-sentencing-until-after-the-election ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/6/trump-wins-2024-us-presidential-election-defeating-kamala-harris ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/1/10/trump-sentenced-to-unconditional-discharge-in-hush-money-case . Date approximations per rule 4: May 2, 2024 entry pegged to AJ's 'Day 10' takeaways article publication date (which was Day 10 of trial proceedings). May 28, 2024 (closing arguments) pegged to AJ piece publication. All other dates correspond to discrete public events (jury verdict, immunity ruling, assassination attempts, debates, election, sentencing). Source disagreements: Hush money case framing varies — AJ's coverage emphasizes the prosecution's election-interference framing (the May 2 entry uses 'election-conspiracy framing' as that was AJ's day 10 takeaway language); Trump and his supporters characterized the case as politically motivated. SCOTUS immunity ruling (Jul 1, 2024) — AJ framed it as 'partial immunity for official acts'; CNN framed it as 'broad immunity'; both characterizations are accurate descriptions of the 6-3 ruling, captured per each outlet's framing. The Jul 15, 2024 dismissal of the classified documents case is captured factually; the appeal by Special Counsel Jack Smith was ongoing through 2024 but mooted by Trump's election win. Public figures named: Trump, Harris, Biden, Merchan (all public officials/political figures). E. Jean Carroll appears on the CNN timeline only — she is a public figure of record (author, columnist, public plaintiff in named litigation) — permitted under rule 6. Politically sensitive: cluster spec marks this as politically_sensitive: false, so reporting is factual and event-anchored, but entries are deliberately framed neutrally and avoid characterization of motive or guilt beyond what was legally established.

Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across CNN's 2024 election and Trump criminal trial coverage (per user instruction). Primary anchor source is CNN's 'Trump's election and criminal trial calendars' (https://www.cnn.com/politics/trump-trial-election-calendar-dg), which is a curated timeline article tracking court dates against campaign milestones. Contributing articles: https://www.cnn.com/interactive/2023/07/politics/trump-indictments-criminal-cases/ (context only, predates cluster window) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/02/15/politics/donald-trump-campaign-trial ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/02/29/politics/what-to-know-timing-of-trumps-trial ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/03/25/politics/trump-first-president-trial-analysis ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/04/13/politics/donald-trump-hush-money-trial-explained ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/04/13/politics/trump-trial-schedule ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/04/14/politics/timeline-trump-stormy-daniels-hush-money-dg ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/04/15/politics/hush-money-trial-process-trump-dg ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/donald-trump-hush-money-trial-dg ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/04/29/politics/donald-trump-trial-campaign-trail-2024 ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/05/01/politics/wisconsin-michigan-trump-rallies ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/05/11/politics/trump-schedule-court-campaign ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/trump-guilty-verdict-trial-05-31-24 (the verdict liveblog, dated May 30-31) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/15/politics/2024-campaign-transformed-analysis ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/trump-transition-news-11-15-24 . Additional contributing articles drawn from CNN's broader coverage to anchor non-trial campaign milestones: https://www.cnn.com/2024/01/26/politics/e-jean-carroll-trump-verdict (Jan 26 Carroll defamation verdict) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/01/politics/scotus-trump-immunity-ruling ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/09/10/politics/trump-harris-presidential-debate ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/06/politics/trump-wins-2024-election ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/01/10/politics/trump-sentenced-hush-money ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/01/20/politics/trump-inauguration-47th-president . Date approximations per rule 4: Several CNN URLs are analysis pieces (Feb 15, Feb 29, Mar 25, May 11) where the entry date is pegged to publication date as the moment CNN ran that day's analytical framing of Trump's calendar; events being analyzed (primary calendar, trial scheduling) are continuous rather than discrete-date. May 30, 2024 (verdict): CNN's liveblog URL is dated 05-31 but covers events of May 30-31; entry dated May 30 to anchor on the verdict-delivery day. The Mar 4, 2024 federal election trial start-date scrap is captured implicitly in the Feb 29 entry rather than as a separate event entry, since it was a scheduling change rather than a discrete trial milestone. Source disagreements: SCOTUS immunity ruling framing — CNN used 'broad immunity' while AJ used 'partial immunity for official acts'; both characterizations are technically accurate of the 6-3 ruling; CNN's framing emphasized the practical breadth, captured per outlet's wording. Trump's 'sweeping all seven battleground states' (Nov 5 entry) — this was the final reported outcome by Nov 6-7 once results were called; CNN's election-day coverage initially showed a developing count, so the entry uses the final reported outcome per CNN's wrap-up coverage. Public figures named: Trump, Harris, Biden, Vance, Merchan, E. Jean Carroll — all public officials/figures of record. The Jan 26 Carroll entry names her because she is a public plaintiff in named litigation and a long-time public author/columnist; permitted under rule 6.
